title: Diversa Trustees Limited — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: APRA imposed additional licence conditions on Diversa Trustees Limited, trustee for 10 registrable superannuation entities covering approximately 291,000 members and $15 billion in funds under management, effective 23 December 2025, after a thematic review found deficiencies in member onboarding, investment monitoring and conflict-of-interest management. Separately, ASIC commenced Federal Court civil penalty proceedings (8 December 2025) alleging Diversa - through which approximately $300 million was invested by members into the collapsed First Guardian Master Fund between 2020 and 2024 - failed to conduct adequate due diligence and ongoing monitoring, and failed to enforce its own 50% investment holding limit.
scope: Subject of additional APRA licence conditions following a thematic review finding onboarding and monitoring deficiencies; Subject of ongoing ASIC Federal Court civil penalty proceedings alleging inadequate due diligence over ~$300 million invested into a collapsed fund; Alleged to have failed to enforce its own investment concentration limit
